ops.body: 2026-07-19 W4a RESULT — "much larger" is REFUTED at the affordable arm. qwen3.6-35b-a3b (35B-total/3B-active MoE, the largest Qwen-family base under the $40/unit cap at ~$19 est) scored sentinel AUC 0.9230 on the uniform Tinker-frame instrument — LAST place: 9B 0.9371 > 8B 0.9301 (deployed) > 4B 0.9262 > 35B-A3B 0.9230. Same E8 recipe (rank 32, lr 1e-4, epochs 2, batch 16, seed 1729); one deviation, max_tokens 4096→8192, forced by the Qwen3.6 tokenizer overflowing one eval row (row set identical — the instrument is unchanged; OverlongEvalRows fail-fast caught it). Caveats: the E8 recipe was tuned on 8B dense — a MoE may want different hyperparameters (a campaign arm could probe that); the "much larger DENSE" question (Qwen3.6-27B, ~$75/unit) remains unmeasured and is over the cap. Run artifacts: ~/.cc-steer/sweep/2026-07-19/qwen3.6-35b-a3b/ (score_report.json + .athome-metric.json), spend ≤ the $30 harness cap (~50 min wall on the athome pipelined engine). Prerequisites landed en route: athome 90362b5 (pinned BASE_MODELS arm) + 282a037 (Tinker price-sheet refresh — the old sheet under-metered 8B/9B ~1.5x vs the live pricing page; all rows now source-verified). Implication for the serving decision: the hosted lane's near-term value is capturing the 9B's +0.7pt (it cannot serve locally via mlx-lm LoRA), not a much-larger model; the W4b Modal spike (hosted 8B artifact vs the 0.9291 local baseline) proceeds to prove the serving stack either way.
